protocol: environmental - heat exposure/heat exhaustion environmental - heat stroke (protocol 5 - 2)

dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond to a sick person at --Address Redacted--.
Hello, I'm Medic Garner with the rescue squad. Can you tell me what brought you to call 911?
Tingling on the left side of my face started this morning, and my left leg is swelling.
Have you had chest pain, shortness of breath, or one-sided weakness?
No chest pain, no shortness of breath, and no weakness on one side.
Are you aware of who you are, where you are, today's date, and the reason you're here?
Yes, I know all of that. I'm not confused.
That means you're alert and oriented to person, place, time, and event-GCS 15 recorded.
Airway is patent-no signs of obstruction or unusual sounds.
Breathing is regular and unlabored with a rate of 18.
Your skin is warm and dry with no cyanosis present.
Your pulse is 78 and regular throughout the exam.
You're breathing steadily at 18 breaths per minute.
Your blood pressure is 140 over 77 today.
Oxygen saturation is 96% on room air.
Blood sugar is currently 90 mg/dL.
I'm placing 4-lead EKG to assess heart function.
Temperature is 98.2°F-everything seems stable so far.
A 12-lead EKG was obtained-currently shows normal sinus rhythm with left axis deviation.
How long has the face tingling and leg swelling been going on?
Tingling began this morning during breakfast. Swelling has been present for a while.
Did anything happen before this started-like a fall or new medication?
No, it was just part of my morning routine. I take new medications sometimes.
Any pain associated with your left leg swelling?
No pain, just swelling that hasn't improved after medication adjustments.
You mentioned Lasix-can you tell me other meds you take?
I take Plavix, Aspirin, Coreg, Celexa, Zyprexa, Lipitor, Protonix, Tegretol, Zofran, Buspar, Mirtazapine, and hydrochlorothiazide.
Any allergies to medications or foods?
No known drug allergies.
What medical conditions or surgeries have you had?
COPD, GERD, hypertension, hyperlipidema, anemia, and schizoaffective disorder.
I also have a history of stroke-like symptoms and had a stent placed.
Your EKG showed left axis deviation. When did you first notice this?
No, that came up during your testing. I don't know about that.
I'm noting marked edema on your left lower extremity.
Pupils are equal, round, and reactive to light-PERRL.
Trachea is midline. No evidence of neck injury.
Breath sounds are equal and normal bilaterally.
No distension, rigidity, or tenderness.
Motor and sensory function in all extremities is intact.
I'm making sure the stretcher is properly set up for you.
You are ambulatory and alert. You've agreed to go to [COMPANY_NAME].
You were safely moved to the stretcher with no issues.
[COMPANY_NAME] has been confirmed as your destination.
We are following the environmental heat exposure protocol for monitoring.
You denied chest pain, shortness of breath, and stroke symptoms. Stable during the exam.
We will keep monitoring your vital signs and heart rhythm throughout transport.
Rechecking your vitals-still 140/77 and 78.
Oxygen satellite at 96%, blood sugar 90-no changes detected.
The 12-lead EKG remains stable with normal sinus rhythm.
ST segments are normal-no elevation or depression noted.
All seatbelts and restraints are in place and functioning.
We've ensured a complete and accurate handoff report is ready for the hospital team.
The ER nurse has been briefed with a full patient summary.
I've documented that your pain level remained 0 throughout the care.
Your medications-Pantoprazole and Promethazine-were also included in our report.
A neurological exam was performed and remains negative for stroke signs.
The patient had no decline in neurological function during transport.
Oxygen and medications were withheld per your report and approval.
Atorvastatin use is noted in your system, as well as your other medication history.
All documentation has been handed to hospital staff for continuity of care.
Your vital signs have remained well-controlled according to ePCR times.
You remain safe and alert with good motor strength on all four sides.
We noted that you have no tenderness and no trapped air signs in the chest.
Skin turgor was not assessed, but no abnormalities were reported per the ePCR.
No swelling in your right leg. Only left lower leg swelling per the patient.
The team noted no issues with your breathing or neurological function during transport.
We advised you to follow up with your ER physician for further testing.
The EMT team ensured all concerns were well-communicated and properly reported.
Your vital signs matched the primary assessment throughout the examination.
Nothing unusual reported from scene staff-at least until now.
The scene staff were noted to have unprofessional conduct during the assessment.
We addressed the misconduct and are preparing an incident report later this evening.
My partner and I have followed protocol thoroughly for each step taken.
Documentation and patient care were handled according to protocol and state standards.
All steps were objectively reported based on direct patient assessment.
You remain alert and oriented, with no neurological changes during transport.
Swelling and other symptoms have remained unchanged for proper care handing.
We are satisfied with the care and communication during the transport.
Everything appears documented and coherent for the ER team's review.
No additional interventions were needed or recommended for this case.
We've confirmed all necessary data was included in the medical report.
You were safely delivered and remained stable for the entire transport phase.
Care was transferred and accepted by the hospital team without incident.
The call has been fully documented and cleaned up appropriately.
A full incident report to be submitted separately about the scene staff behavior.
The patient remains without signs of acute deterioration.
Thank you for your cooperation and for answering our questions patiently.
We'll see you soon at the ER and hope to learn more from their assessment.
Please don't hesitate to call if you have a return of symptoms.
We'll leave the ER team handling everything from this point forward.
You've made it safely to the hospital and into their care.
We did everything required by standing orders and your requests.
Thanks again for your cooperation and for being alert and open.
We're cleared and ready for the next call.
